# Dependencies: Form 1040 Line 32 — Total Other Payments and Refundable Credits

> Tax year 2025. Re-authored 2026-06-14 to match current program logic.
> Authoritative source: `lines/32.md`.

---

## 1. Outputs

| Output field | Java model | Form 1040 destination |
|---|---|---|
| `Payments.totalOtherPaymentsAndRefundableCredits` | `Payments.java` | Line 32 (= 27a + 28 + 29 + 30 + 31) |

Storage convention: **null-when-zero via ternary-at-setter** (Convention 1) at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:27290-27291`:

```java
payments.setTotalOtherPaymentsAndRefundableCredits(
    line32.compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) > 0 ? line32 : null);
```

---

## 2. Top-Level Formula

```text
Form1040.line32 = line27a + line28 + line29 + line30 + line31

  line27a = earned income credit
  line28  = additional child tax credit (Schedule 8812 Part II-A)
  line29  = refundable American opportunity credit (Form 8863 line 8)
  line30  = refundable adoption credit (Form 8839 Part II line 13)
  line31  = Schedule 3 line 15
```

Withholding (line 25d) and estimated tax (line 26) are **NOT** in line 32 — they are added separately at line 33. Mixing them in would double-count at the line-33 pivot.

---

## 3. Inputs (5 Addends — All Upstream-Computed)

### 3a. Direct addend fields read by line 32

| Addend | Computed by | `Payments.java` field | Form 1040 line |
|---|---|---|---|
| EIC | `computeLine27aEIC()` (inside `computeLine31ThroughLine38`) | `earnedIncomeCredit` | 27a |
| ACTC | `computeSchedule8812()` Part II-A | `additionalChildTaxCredit` | 28 |
| Refundable AOTC | `computeForm8863()` line 8 | `americanOpportunityCredit` | 29 |
| Refundable Adoption | `computeAdoptionBenefits()` Part II + `applyAdoptionCredit()` | `refundableAdoptionCredit` | 30 |
| Schedule 3 line 15 | `finalizeSchedule3Totals()` → line 31 wiring | `otherPaymentsSchedule3` | 31 |

### 3b. Statement inputs that indirectly feed line 32 components

| Statement type | Field | Reaches line 32 via |
|---|---|---|
| `w-2` | `wagesTipsOtherCompAmount` (box 1) | Line 27a EIC earned income |
| `w-2` | `box12CodeT` entries | Line 30 (Form 8839 Part III employer benefits) |

(Withholding fields on W-2 box 2 / 1099-series box 4 do NOT feed line 32 — they go to line 25d → line 33.)

### 3c. Personal form inputs that affect line 32 components

| Form ID | Field | Affects |
|---|---|---|
| `earned-income-credit-taxpayer` / `-spouse` | `claimsEIC`, `otherEarnedIncome`, `electNontaxableCombatPay` | Line 27a |
| `adoption-expenses` | `adoptionCreditPartII.*`, `imports.magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion` | Line 30 (MAGI phaseout) |
| `31-other-payments` | `hasFuelTaxCredit`, `hasSection1341Credit`, `hasDeferred965Tax`, `hasOtherRefundableCredits` and their value fields | Line 31 via Schedule 3 Part II |

---

## 4. Compute Order

Line 32 can only be assembled after all 5 component lines are finalized:

```text
1.  computeAdoptionBenefits()            # early tentative refundableAdoptionCredit
2.  computeForm8863()                    # form8863.line8RefundableAotc (line 29 pre-set)
3.  computeSchedule8812()                # ACTC (line 28); pre-set early for Part II-B
4.  applyAdoptionCredit()                # confirms refundableAdoptionCredit; Schedule 3 line 6c
5.  applyPremiumTaxCreditToSchedule3()   # Schedule 3 line 9
6.  applyForm4868ToSchedule3()           # Schedule 3 line 10
7.  computeExcessSocialSecurityTax()     # Schedule 3 line 11
8.  applyForm2439CreditToSchedule3()     # Schedule 3 line 13a
9.  applyOtherPaymentsFormToSchedule3()  # Schedule 3 lines 12, 13b, 13c, 13d, 13z
10. finalizeSchedule3Totals()            # Schedule 3 lines 14 + 15 → otherPaymentsSchedule3
11. computeLine20ThroughLine24()         # line 22 taxAfterCredits (needed by Form 2210)
12. computeLine31ThroughLine38() {
       compute line 25d / 26 / 27a (re-confirm) / 29 / 30 / 31
       *** compute line 32 here (lines 27270-27291) ***
       compute line 33 / 34 / 35a-d / 36 / 37
    }
```

Inline wiring at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:27270-27291`:

```java
BigDecimal line32 = safeAmount(payments.getEarnedIncomeCredit())          // 27a
                  .add(safeAmount(payments.getAdditionalChildTaxCredit())) // 28
                  .add(safeAmount(payments.getAmericanOpportunityCredit()))// 29
                  .add(safeAmount(payments.getRefundableAdoptionCredit())) // 30
                  .add(safeAmount(payments.getOtherPaymentsSchedule3()));  // 31
// non-negativity guardrail (32.md Gap G) — logs WARN if any addend < 0
payments.setTotalOtherPaymentsAndRefundableCredits(
    line32.compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) > 0 ? line32 : null);
```

`safeAmount()` treats null as zero so the sum is well-defined even when component lines are absent.

---

## 5. Non-Negativity Guardrail (32.md Gap G)

Each addend is non-negative by IRS construction. The backend installs a defensive paper-trail guardrail at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:27281-27289`:

```text
if (any addend < 0 OR line32 < 0):
    LOG.warnf("Line 32 non-negativity guardrail tripped — line27a=… line28=… line29=… line30=… line31=… line32=…")
```

Computation continues — this is a paper trail, NOT a hard block. It surfaces upstream regressions where a refundable-credit helper accidentally emits a negative value. Mirrored at line 33 (lines 27306-27312).

---

## 6. MFS Mechanism — M2 Transitive Inheritance

Line 32 has no per-spouse code or MFS-specific logic. All 5 addends handle MFS protection upstream via M2 transitive inheritance from their respective sub-line MFS guards. Line 32 is a pure 5-addend summation; MFS segregation happens before line 32 is formed.

Pattern: M2 (transitive).

---

## 7. Downstream Consumers

| Consumer | Reads |
|---|---|
| `Payments.totalPayments` (line 33) | `totalOtherPaymentsAndRefundableCredits` |
| `Refund.overpaid` (line 34) | line 33 − line 24 (transitive via line 33) |
| `AmountOwed.amountOwed` (line 37) | line 24 − line 33 (transitive via line 33) |
| Form 2210 line 4 (refundable credits) | line 27a + 28 + 29 + 30 (NOT line 31) — `computeForm2210()` |
| Form 1040 PDF — `line32_total_other_payments_refundable_credits` | `payments.totalOtherPaymentsAndRefundableCredits` |
| Frontend `line32OtherPayments()` | prefers stored backend value; falls back to client-side sum for backward compat |

---

---

## 9. Blocking Flags That Can Zero Out Component Lines

| Flag | Component zeroed | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| `FORM_8862_EIC_REQUIRED` | line 27a | Previously denied EIC + no Form 8862 |
| `FORM_8862_CTC_REQUIRED` | line 28 (ACTC) | Previously denied CTC + no Form 8862 |
| `FORM_8862_AOTC_REQUIRED` | line 29 | Previously denied AOTC + no Form 8862 |
| Form 2555 filer present | lines 28 and 29 | `form2555Taxpayer != null || form2555Spouse != null` |
| `EDUCATION_CREDITS_MFS_INELIGIBLE` | line 29 | MFS filer |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MFS_EXCLUSION_DISALLOWED` | line 30 | MFS without special-needs exception |
| MAGI > $299,190 | line 30 | Adoption credit fully phased out |

Line 32 itself emits no flags — zeroing happens upstream at the component-line helpers.

---

## 10. Key Validation Rules

- `line32 = line27a + line28 + line29 + line30 + line31` exactly; no other addends
- `line32 ≥ 0` (defensive guardrail logs WARN if any addend < 0)
- Nonrefundable credits must NOT be pulled into line 32 (Part I → line 20)
- Withholding (25d) and estimated tax (26) must NOT be pulled into line 32 (they belong on line 33)
- If a component is blocked / ineligible / opted out upstream, it must become null/zero before line 32 is formed
